current.note: P/B of 0.955x implies either (a) Ke~14% with ROE ~13.6% sustainable, or (b) Ke~10.5% with ROE staying at a distressed ~10% level. Interpretation (a) is the market's actual thesis -- crisis Ke at 14% plus no recovery assumption. Interpretation (b) would be a distress scenario not consistent with Fitch AA-(idn) stable.

target_mean.note: Street implies Ke ~13% (pre-hike) and ROE stable at 14-15%. The street agrees ROE does not decline materially but does not price a ROE recovery.

target_high.note: High target (1 analyst) implies ROE recovering toward 15-16% -- closer to our base thesis.

target_low.note: Low target (UBS equivalent range) implies continued ROE depression -- UBS's actual PT is 745 IDR.

justified_grid.description: Justified P/B = (ROE - g) / (Ke - g). Implied IDR value per share = justified P/B x BV (722.3). g = 5.5% fixed.

grid_idr.description: IDR per share = justified P/B x 722.3
grid_idr.base_case_cell: Ke=10.5%, ROE=17% (nearest to our 16.5% base): 1,661 IDR -- consistent with blended DDM/ER output of 1,357 IDR (ER dilutes the DDM and JPB)
grid_idr.current_price_note: ALL cells are above the current price of 690 IDR. Even the bear cell (Ke=11.5%, ROE=14%) = 1,023 IDR = 48% above current price. This confirms the valuation is asymmetrically favorable.

peers.name: Adira Finance
peers.premium_discount_to_justified: DISCOUNT -- at 0.64x vs 0.96x justified, Adira screens cheap but NI -27% in 2024 justifies the discount. Adira has worse credit trajectory than BFIN.

peers.name: Clipan Finance
peers.premium_discount_to_justified: ROE below any reasonable Ke -- perpetual value destroyer at current profitability. Not a useful comp.

peers.name: BRI (bank)
peers.premium_discount_to_justified: DISCOUNT -- BRI at 1.32x vs justified ~2.8x at Ke=9.5% (lower Ke because deposit-funded, lower risk). BRI is also undervalued on fundamentals. This suggests the entire Indonesian financial sector is pricing crisis Ke, not fundamental Ke.

peer_comparison.sector_observation: The entire Indonesian financial sector appears to be pricing crisis Ke (14%+) relative to fundamental Ke (10-11%). BRI at 1.32x P/B (ROE 18%) and BFIN at 0.93x P/B (ROE 14.8%) are both at deep discounts to justified P/B on fundamental Ke assumptions. This is a sector-wide repricing of Indonesian financial risk, driven by BI's aggressive rate cycle (100bp in 30 days) and rupiah at record weakness. Our BUY on BFIN is a bet that this crisis Ke is temporary and that normalized Ke eventually reasserts.

reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 690 IDR (P/B 0.955x), the market prices in a persistent crisis: either ROE stays at ~13.5% (with no recovery from the 2022-2025 compression) at a crisis Ke of 14%, OR ROE recovers to ~10% at our fundamental Ke of 10.5%. The former is the market's most plausible implicit assumption. The sub-book valuation is NOT pricing a default or NPF blowout -- Fitch AA-(idn) stable and NPF 1.25% (vs sector 2.70%) make distress remote. It is pricing a 'new normal' of lower ROE and higher Ke in the rate-elevated Indonesian macro environment.
reconstruction.what_street_implies: The mean analyst target of 860 IDR (P/B 1.19x) implies Ke of ~13% (the pre-hike baseline from 2024) and ROE stable at 14-15%. The street has not updated its Ke assumption for the 100bp BI rate surprise in May-June 2026. UBS (the sole sell-side bear, PT 745) is more internally consistent: it has updated its credit cost and growth forecasts for the adverse environment. The 2 buy + 1 hold analysts are implicitly using stale Ke assumptions.
reconstruction.history_anchor: BFIN historically traded at 1.0x-1.8x P/B (2018-2022 range) when ROE was 18-22%. The 0.93x P/B is the lowest level in recent history, consistent with a significant repricing from peak. The P/B-to-ROE relationship has been consistent: 20% ROE commanded 1.5-1.8x P/B; 15% ROE commanded 1.0-1.2x P/B. The current 14.8% ROE should logically command ~1.0x P/B at pre-crisis Ke -- and the stock is at 0.93x P/B. The crisis Ke is the overhang.
reconstruction.contested_lever: Ke is the crux, not ROE. The market and our fundamental Ke disagree by 350bp (14% vs 10.5%). This Ke gap alone moves the justified P/B from 0.93x (where the stock trades) to 2.18x (our fundamental). ROE recovery is the second lever -- the market assumes flat ROE; we assume recovery to 16.5%. But the Ke disagreement is larger in value terms.
reconstruction.our_disagree: We disagree with the market on TWO fronts: (1) Ke should normalize from crisis 14% to fundamental 10.5% as BI hike cycle ends and rupiah stabilizes; (2) ROE will recover toward 16.5% as provision normalizes and GP margin recovers. Both disagreements favor the BUY. We partially agree with the street (860 IDR mean target) on the near-term traded level -- our 12m target of 910 IDR is close to the street's 860 IDR, because neither the street nor we expect full intrinsic value realization in 12 months.
reconstruction.ubs_engage: UBS's bear thesis (PT 745, Sell) is the most rigorous dissent. UBS argues credit costs of 4.6-5.1% for 2025-27 -- our base uses 2.5% of receivables (lower). Q1 2026 credit cost data will be the first real test: if Q2 2026 cost of credit annualizes above 4%, UBS is right and our normalization thesis is wrong. Our counterargument: the Q1 2025 credit cost spike that triggered UBS's downgrade (4.4%, up 160bp QoQ) was partly seasonal and partly driven by the post-COVID restructuring vintage burning through. The 9M 2024 NPF at 1.25% gross (best in sector) is not consistent with sustained 4.6-5.1% credit costs. UBS's 2.5% loan CAGR is already contradicted by FY2025 new financing +9.3%.
